transcript.text: For example, with argument is that there's kind of two paths here, right?
And we discussed last week was do we want to have a path where we're just optimizing for speed just to prove out the value and show the signals and show this thing working?
There's a second path, which is like you get approval on your end to throw this in a subdomain and we make this look more like a bolt instance, you know?
And so then the domain is not even relevant at all.
And then the third would be putting it in a subdirectory, which would be the ideal from an SEO and, you know, and integrate it more with the bolt experience on the homepage a bit more, right?
Like, at least link to it a bit more.
But even just having it as a subdirectory would go like, be like massive, right?
So if you, you know, what VZero and Lovable are doing essentially where like the community stuff is pretty, pretty in your face.
It's impossible to miss essentially.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I mean, I think, I think, uh, for us doing something like, um,
Like, orphaned pages on the main domain is probably what I would like to go with.

So that's where, like, having the data model so that we could know what we would need to build as far as what are the different pieces, right, that would plug into whatever kind of we put on the site.
I think that's kind of the main thing.
So you're saying, like, sorry, subdomain or subdirectory?
A directory.
it'll be on our main domain slash, you know, templates slash whatever is kind of how I'm envisioning it because I think that that is going to give us the best results from an SEO perspective.
And then that's, you know, the best opportunity for us to show that, like, this was a good partnership, actually.
Yeah, yeah.

Okay, so what do you think, let's call it in the next four weeks would be, and it's okay if the answer is like, sorry, dude, we're just going to take this, get as much value as we can, we paid good money and call it a day.
Or do you see this as like, should we in our mental model be, let's set what we want in the next four weeks to have done and shipped and proven in order to continue?
Or you're thinking more of like, and it's okay either way, right?
I just want to, so that I can, there's kind of two operate, modes of operated operations.
One is it goes super fast, so there's enough signals so that in four weeks, like we can prove this out and then figure out the like the long term, sustainable.
And versus there's also the like, get this to as handoffish as possible in the next four weeks versus, you know, like, so those are just slightly different ways that we can then opt.
Yeah, so it would probably be the handoff.
don't see internally people being open to continuing is the vibe.
So, and then, like, I think, for example, like this, I think this looks really good.
This obviously was built with V0, not Bolt.
Like, that's another thing that is going to, like, I can't show this to anyone internally, because they're not going to like that.
I personally don't care, because I'm like, let's get something done and, like, start getting results.

But that's just another thing where it's like, okay, like, this is, you know, not going to be, it's just something that if I, like, go show people, they're going to be like, yeah, like, we're definitely not working with these people ever again, kind of thing.
Yeah.
They're not even, like, using our own product to build the stuff that we're asking them to build.
So, Yeah.
Which, by the way, the framing itself is, but then the website itself, this is the next thing I was going to show you.
this is an example of one of the websites that we built, and this is fully on Bolt.
It's mostly in one shot, pretty much.
Totally, totally.
I get what you're saying.
But you're right.
Based on our conversation last week, was optimized for getting the right site and the right experience in place.
Yeah, just move.
So I'm totally fine with that, but it's the kind of thing that people internally would not like.
Yeah, that makes sense.
Yeah, that makes sense.
Okay, cool.
So let us regroup internally.
I think what we could do is turn this over the next week as a more Bolt branded, you know.
Repo, or try to replicate this in, you know, using Bolt itself, you know.
If it's easier and faster for you guys in V0, like, feel free.
And as long as we have a repo at the end of the day, that's what I care about.
It's something that we can, like, look at and, you know, see what we can do there.
Like I said, the, yeah, that's what's most important to me, and I want to, yeah.
So whatever helps you guys move faster, feel free.
Okay, perfect.
That sounds good.
Okay, so I'll follow up with the details.

So what I'm hearing is as we wrap up the project, the handoffs over the next three, four weeks before that we wrap up the project will be a repo with an experience that's going to look a lot more like Bolt that you can put it in a subdirectory.
One, two, like, just details on the overall data model of those pages, you know.
And what we're proposing, and a few sample pages with sample websites, essentially, and a sample prompt, you know, that we can hand off.
Yeah, so what does that look like as far as number of pages that we're actually putting here?
I guess my question is, what are we actually, like, what's the result here?
Like, is it the site that you're building, or is it the actual, like, generated pages?

Yeah, like, I think there's, like, a bit of a disconnect, because, you know, it's easy to look at this and go, this is the site, but it's, like, it's informed by a lot of insight, a lot of research, a lot of, like, the workflows we're, like, that we're going to inform, like, scaling this up.
But obviously, like, you all can do that as well.

And so that, like, the insight here is that, and that's what I think is the most valuable piece, the fact that there's, like, we figure out what the use case was, which your data science team, you know, didn't have as much clarity.
We process a ton of session data and figure out that lending pages and website templates were a huge use case that we can double down on.
Then we figured out that, hey, there are long-tail keywords.
For instance, notary website templates gets 1,800 searches a month and has a keyword difficulty of eight.
There is literally millions and millions of searches of long-tail website templates for every single category possible.
And there's very little competition for it.
And so then it just becomes, how do you scale this to thousands and thousands of templates that are actually really, really, really good?
And that was the whole engagement, right?

Like this figuring out long-term was going to be to essentially start to scale that with the humans in the loop and start to produce these templates, but also start to build these as starter kits so that these templates are a lot more like they start from a common place.
So it's not like starting from a blink.
Like prompt.

Ideally, it's like you take a repo that already has a bit of the front end best practices baked in, and then like you give it a plan to like adapt it to a different brand, different use case, shove a different like, you know, content into it, right?
And then create the descriptions.
And so the, like what, what I would do in your case from this handoff is to say like, hey, now we need to go hire somebody to go scale this out, right?

Like, and versus like, which you have the strategy, the website built out, the template, you can connect to your own, or we can migrate, you know, our super base instance or transfer ownership to that.

So it should be pretty plug and play as far as like, go crank this out, essentially, you know, short of like, we'll go crank this out in four weeks, because we got to get it to a deliverability and, you know, and publishing the site.
Okay.
so, yeah.

So I think that it would be great if you could put together a list of like, these are the deliverables that we're handing off to you guys, just so that we're very clear on what that will look like, and then go from there.
Yeah, that sounds good.
Cool.
All right.
Cool, man.
Well, I appreciate it.
We'll follow up.
Okay, sounds good.
Thank you.
Bye-bye.
Bye-bye.
Bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss project handoff strategy and deliverables for StackBlitz template generation project.](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [StackBlitz prefers subdirectory implementation (/templates/) on main domain for SEO benefits](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=95.0)
  - [Project to wrap up in \~4 weeks; focus on handoff-ready deliverables](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=136.0)
  - [Need to migrate from V0 to Bolt for internal StackBlitz approval](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=204.0)
  - [Clarification needed on final deliverables; Marcel to provide detailed list](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=389.0)

## Topics

### Implementation Strategy

  - [Three potential paths discussed:](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)
    1.  Speed-optimized proof of concept
    2.  Subdomain implementation (bolt-like instance)
    3.  Subdirectory implementation (preferred)
  - [StackBlitz favors subdirectory (/templates/) on main domain for SEO](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=95.0)
  - [Importance of making community content highly visible (cf. VZero, Lovable)](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=44.0)

### Project Timeline and Expectations

  - [\~4 week timeframe for project completion](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=122.0)
  - [Focus on creating handoff-ready deliverables](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=136.0)
  - [StackBlitz team not likely open to continuing partnership long-term](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)
  - [Need for clear, itemized list of deliverables from GrowthX](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=389.0)

### Technical Considerations

  - [Current demo built with V0, not Bolt (StackBlitz's product)](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=201.0)
  - [Internal StackBlitz team sensitive to use of non-Bolt tools](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=204.0)
  - [GrowthX to migrate/rebuild demo using Bolt for better reception](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=249.0)
  - [Mitchell open to V0 if it expedites process; prioritizes having a workable repo](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=307.0)

### Value Proposition and Insights

  - [GrowthX provided valuable insights through research and data analysis](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=409.0)
  - [Identified website templates (esp. niche types) as high-potential use case](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=409.0)
  - [Example: "notary website templates" - 1,800 monthly searches, keyword difficulty of 8](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=434.0)
  - [Millions of long-tail searches for various website template categories](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=442.0)
  - [Strategy involves scaling to thousands of high-quality templates](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=454.0)
  - [Proposed workflow: Start with best-practice frontend repo, adapt for different use cases](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)

### Deliverables Discussion

  - [Repo with Bolt-like experience, implementable in subdirectory](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
  - [Details on overall data model for pages](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=353.0)
  - [Sample pages with example websites](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=249.0)
  - [Sample prompts for generation](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [Potentially transferable Supabase instance](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0)
  - [Comprehensive strategy for scaling template generation](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=454.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Marcel to compile and send detailed list of project deliverables](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=334.0)
  - [GrowthX to migrate demo from V0 to Bolt](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=249.0)
  - [Finalize handoff strategy and materials over next 3-4 weeks](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
  - [StackBlitz to review deliverables list and confirm expectations](https://fathom.video/share/1WzGLYiRiAjTqe9eBBVVkRq_9Y_fM9LV?tab=summary&timestamp=541.0)

action_items.description: Convert current site to more Bolt-branded repo OR replicate site using Bolt
Prepare handoff materials (repo, data model, samples) for subdirectory implementation
Create list of deliverables for handoff (repo w/ Bolt-like exp, data model details, sample pages/websites, sample prompt)
